Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres

15 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Portrait of a Young Man Standing on the Quirinal with the Turris Comitum in the Background
by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres
France, 19th century · 1800s · graphite
The Ponte dell'Abbadia at Vulci
by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres
France, 19th century · first half 19th century · graphite
Jacques Marguet de Norvins
by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres
France, 19th century · c. 1811 · lithograph
La Grande Odalisque
by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres
French Neoclassicism · 1814 · Louvre, Paris
A concubine reclines on impossibly long, boneless anatomy. Critics counted extra vertebrae; Ingres was not interested in bones — he was interested in line.
Frederic 5th Earl of Guilford
by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres
France, 19th century · 1815 · lithograph

Amédée-David, the Comte de Pastoret
by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres
France · 19th century · 1823–1826 · Oil on canvas

by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres
